'''Dunari''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] entrepreneur who was an old friend of [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas Azzameen]] and the founder and owner of [[Dunari's Rest]] Casino during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was also the leader of a powerful and large trading coalition based in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], mainly the [[Mayagil sector/Legends|Mayagil sector]].

==Biography==
A smuggler in his younger days and a friend of the [[Azzameen family]], it was known that Dunari and Tomaas used to make [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Runs]] with [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] in hot pursuit. He would recall that what they both lacked in finesse they made up with luck. He had his own [[Muurian transport]] called ''[[Deadman's Hand]]''.

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] destroyed the Rebels' main supply center on [[Gal Milnor]] late in the war, as result the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was in dire need of resources and supplies and called on Dunari and his coalition to provide them, a call he accepted.

After Dunari arrived to the meeting in his his personal [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[Personal Luxury Yacht 3000/Legends|Personal Luxury Yacht 3000]] ''[[Raw Deal]]'' to meet the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—who had sent a [[Muurian transport]] ''[[Saloon]]''—he negotiated a deal for his trading coalition to secretly supply the Rebels for their [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the [[Death Star II/Legends|Second Death Star]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. But he didn't expect the [[Hurrim]], led by [[Golov Nakhym]], to betray them at the [[Nezni system/Legends|Nezni system]] by firing on innocent civilians. Dunari then removed the Hurrim from his coalition and placed [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] on their heads.

He also helped [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] to rescue his brother, [[Emon Azzameen]], who was [[Prisoner/Legends|captured]] by the Imperials and was being held at [[Quesna Base]].

Soon Dunari's casino was attacked by Imperials tipped of by the Hurrim. But the attack was repelled and Dunari was able to get the guests and the personnel, along with Emon out of the casino and into safety.

Fed up with the Hurrim, the Alliance and Dunari's forces launched a full-scale assault on the Hurrim's nearby asteroid base, rescuing Dunari's men and destroying the station, while capturing the notorious [[Golov Nakhym]]. During the battle, Admiral [[Garreth Holtz]] and the [[Corrupter (Imperial-class)]] were destroyed.

With the Hurrim defeated and the local Imperials in disarray, Dunari's supply operation for the Alliance was able to continue unimpeded and played a critical role in insuring the Rebels had the resources they needed for their successful [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|new Death Star]].

Shortly before the battle, he also helped get Emon, Ace and [[Aeron Azzameen|Aeron]] out of an Imperial [[Mission to Kessel Station|ambush]] at [[Kessel Station]] after [[Antan Azzameen|Antan]], their uncle, had betrayed them.
